title: Bot group messaging method
abstract: Methods, apparatuses, and computing systems are provided for bot messaging. In an implementation, a method may include one or more of receiving, by a group messaging service coupled to the internet and a group, a message from a user node of the group. The message includes an address and an identification of the group and the 5 user node. The method further includes determining that the group includes a bot, which includes a software application for performing one or more tasks over the internet. The method also includes identifying that the message address corresponds to the bot and sending, by the group messaging service, the message to the bot.
claims:
Claims 1. A method comprising: receiving, by a group messaging service coupled to the internet and a group, message from a user node of the group, the message comprising an address and an identification of the group and the user node; determining that the group comprises a bot, the bot comprising a software application for performing one or more tasks over the internet; identifying that the message address corresponds to the bot; and sending, by the group messaging service, the message to the bot. The method of claim 1, wherein prior to receiving the message, the method further comprising: receiving, by the group messaging service, a request to add the bot to the group; verifying the bot is available over the internet; configuring the bot into the group; and providing a notification that the bot is configured into the group. The method of claim 1, the method further comprising: sending the message to other user nodes of the group, the group comprising the other nodes, in response to one of: determining the group comprises no bots; and determining the group comprises one or more bots and the message address does not correspond to the one or more bots. The method of claim 1, wherein the message comprises recorded audio, a first group identifier, and an identifier of the user node. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the group messaging service comprises a plurality of data structures for a plurality of groups, the plurality of data structures each comprising the user node identifier and a unique second group identifier. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein determining that the group comprises a bot comprising: selecting a data structure of the plurality of data structures, the selected data structure comprising a second group identifier matching the first group identifier; reviewing the data structure for bot entries, each comprising a bot identifier and a bot address; and determining the data structure comprises at least one bot entry. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein identifying that the message address corresponds to the bot comprises: identifying a bot entry in the data structure comprising a bot address corresponding to the message address. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein at least one other data structure of the plurality of data structures does not include the bot entry. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein after sending the message to the bot, the bot is configured to perform the one or more tasks and responsively provide a notification associated with the user node to the group messaging service. The method of claim 9, wherein the group messaging service provides a notification related to the one or more tasks to the user node. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium configured to store instructions that when executed cause a processor to perform: 

 receiving, by a group messaging service coupled to the internet and a group and comprising the processor, a message from a user node of the group, the message comprising an address and an identification of the group and the user node; determining that the group comprises a bot, the bot comprising a software application for performing one or more tasks over the internet; identifying that the message address corresponds to the bot; and sending, by the group messaging service, the message to the bot. The non- transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 11, wherein the message comprises recorded audio, a first group identifier, and an identifier of the user node. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 12, further configured to store a plurality of data structures for a plurality of groups, the plurality of data structures each comprising the user node identifier and a unique second group identifier. 14. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 13, wherein determining that the group comprises a bot comprising: selecting a data structure of the plurality of data structures, the selected data structure comprising a second group identifier matching the first group identifier; reviewing the data structure for bot entries each comprising a bot identifier and a bot address; and determining the data structure comprises at least one bot entry. 15. The non- transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 14, wherein identifying that the message address corresponds to the bot comprises: identifying a bot entry in the data structure comprising a bot address corresponding to the message address. 16. The non- transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein at least one other data structure of the plurality of data structures does not include the bot entry. 17. A computing system, comprising: a storage system comprising program instructions; and a processing system, operably coupled to the storage system, wherein program instructions when executed by the processing system are directed to: receive, by a group messaging service coupled to the internet and a group, a message from a user node of the group, the message comprising an address and an identification of the group and the user node; determine that the group comprises a bot, the bot comprising a software application for performing one or more tasks over the internet; identify that the message address corresponds to the bot; and send the message to the bot. The computing system of claim 17, wherein prior to receiving the message, the processing system configured to: receive, by the group messaging service, a request to add the bot to the group; verify the bot is available over the internet; configure the bot into the group; and provide a notification that the bot is configured into the group. The computing system of claim 17, wherein the message comprises recorded audio, a first group identifier, and an identifier of the user node, wherein the storage system comprises a plurality of data structures for a plurality of groups, the plurality of data structures each comprising the user node identifier and a unique second group identifier. 

 The computing system of claim 19, wherein a data structure of the plurality of data structures comprises a second group identifier matching the first group identifier and a bot entry comprising a bot address corresponding to the message address, wherein at least one other data structure of the plurality of data structures does not include the bot entry.
